title: Fiber-seq for Flash Frozen Mouse Gastroc Tissue for IGVF
author: Jasmine Sakr
materials: Make enough Buffers for the total number of samples and keep on ice. Name Reagent Final conc. 1 2 1 Lysis buffer (LB) Nuclei Isolation/Extraction Buffer NA 4.5 mL 9 mL 2 Resuspension Buffer (RSB) Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) NA 3.45 mL 6.41 mL 3 7.5% BSA 0.1% 46.67 µL 86.67 µL 4 Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) NA 5 mL 10 mL 5 Debris Removal Solution (DRS) Debris Removal Solution (Miltenyi) NA 1 mL 2 mL Or a large stock can be stored at 4ºC for a month.
Make buffers and solutions. Buffer A %s %s 0.5 Molarity (M) Spermidine and store at 4 °C . 20 Mass / % volume Sodium Dodecyl Sulfate (SDS) and store at Room temperature .
Discard the supernatant into a designated waste container.

steps.title: Keep flash frozen tissue samples on dry ice until lysis
steps.instruction: Keep flash frozen tissue samples on dry ice until lysis.

steps.title: Spin 3 million nuclei at 500...
steps.instruction: Spin 3 million nuclei at 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .

steps.title: Set two thermomixers at 56 °C and 25 °C
steps.instruction: Set two thermomixers at 56 °C and 25 °C .

steps.title: Prepare and label the following tubes...
steps.instruction: Prepare and label the following tubes for DNA extraction (for each labeling reaction): Bead strainer in a collection tube 2 mL tube for wash step 2 mL tube for elution step 1.5 tube for final elution Add 100 µL of gDNA Elution Buffer II to the 2 mL elution tubes.

steps.title: Prepare an ice bucket and keep the following on ice
steps.instruction: Prepare an ice bucket and keep the following on ice: Proteinase k and RNAse A 0.5 Molarity (M) Spermidine

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.5 mL Nuclei Extraction Buffer to a chilled gentleMACS C Tube.

steps.title: Drop whole frozen tissue into the...
steps.instruction: Drop whole frozen tissue into the chilled gentleMACS C Tube with 2.5 mL Nuclei Extraction Buffer.

steps.title: Invert immediately , ensuring tissue is...
steps.instruction: Invert immediately , ensuring tissue is not stuck to the bottom or sides. Proceed immediately to load onto the Octo Dissociator and add an Octo Cooler over each gentleMACS C Tube.

steps.title: Run the gentleMACS Program 4C_nuclei_1 on...
steps.instruction: Run the gentleMACS Program 4C_nuclei_1 on the Octo Dissociator ( 00:05:00 ).

steps.title: Remove the gentleMACS C Tube and...
steps.instruction: Remove the gentleMACS C Tube and ensure tissue did not get stuck on the sides by tapping the tube. * If the tissue sample fails to dissociate, try rerunning the program. After the third unsuccessful attempt, remove the tissue from LB and place it in a new 1.5 mL tube. Place the tube in -80 freezer for 5-10 mins to refreeze tissue, and then try again.

steps.title: Spin down in 4 °C centrifuge for 00:00:10
steps.instruction: Spin down in 4 °C centrifuge for 00:00:10 .

steps.title: Mix the nuclei suspension gently and...
steps.instruction: Mix the nuclei suspension gently and thoroughly by pipetting with a 5 mL pipette.

steps.title: Filter the nuclei suspension through 70...
steps.instruction: Filter the nuclei suspension through 70 µm MACS SmartStrainer into a 5 mL tube. Hold your 5 mL pipette perpendicular to the filter. Press your tip against its center and slowly pass the suspension through the filter while moving your tip in a circular motion. Try to minimize the area of the circle around the center point. Follow these instructions for any filtering unless stated otherwise.

steps.title: Wash the empty, used gentleMACS C...
steps.instruction: Wash the empty, used gentleMACS C Tube with an additional 2 mL Nuclei Extraction Buffer . Close the cap and swish to recover any nuclei stuck to the sides.

steps.title: Filter the 2 mL additional Nuclei...
steps.instruction: Filter the 2 mL additional Nuclei Extraction Buffer through the 70 µm MACS SmartStrainer into the same 5 mL tube (total volume now 4.5 mL).

steps.title: Centrifuge the 4
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the 4.5 mL nuclei suspension at 700 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .

steps.title: Remove the supernatant without disrupting the pellet
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant without disrupting the pellet.

steps.title: Resuspend nuclei pellet in 3
steps.instruction: Resuspend nuclei pellet in 3.1 mL RSB. Mix by pipetting up and down 7-10 times using a 5 mL pipette. Performing this step poorly might influence the accuracy of the nuclei count and, consequently, the input into the next step.

steps.title: Filter nuclei suspension through 30 µm...
steps.instruction: Filter nuclei suspension through 30 µm MACS SmartStrainer into a 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Make aliquots of 3 million nuclei in 1
steps.instruction: Make aliquots of 3 million nuclei in 1.5 mL tubes and proceed to labeling and DNA extraction.

steps.title: During the 5 min spin, mix...
steps.instruction: During the 5 min spin, mix the following and keep the Reaction Master Mix On ice . Buffer A + Spermidine Room temperature %s %s Reaction Master Mix (75 U) On ice %s %s

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 180 µL of Reaction Master Mix .

steps.title: Incubate on 25 °C heat block for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate on 25 °C heat block for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Add 2 DNA capture beads to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 2 DNA capture beads to each sample.

steps.title: Calculate and add 0
steps.instruction: Calculate and add 0.7x volume isopropanol to each sample.

steps.title: Mix on a vertical rotating mixer...
steps.instruction: Mix on a vertical rotating mixer at 10 rpm, °C, 10 minutes to attach DNA to the beads.

steps.title: During the wash steps, work quickly...
steps.instruction: During the wash steps, work quickly to prevent DNA from drying out on beads!

steps.title: Discard liquid by keeping tube upright,...
steps.instruction: Discard liquid by keeping tube upright, insert pipette tip and gently push beads aside to remove liquid.

steps.title: Add 500 µL Wash Buffer
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL Wash Buffer .

steps.title: Mix by gently inverting the tube 3 times
steps.instruction: Mix by gently inverting the tube 3 times.

steps.title: Discard liquid by keeping tube upright,...
steps.instruction: Discard liquid by keeping tube upright, insert pipette tip and gently push beads aside to remove liquid.

steps.title: Add 500 µL Wash Buffer
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL Wash Buffer .

steps.title: Mix by gently inverting the tube 3 times
steps.instruction: Mix by gently inverting the tube 3 times.

steps.title: Discard liquid by keeping tube upright,...
steps.instruction: Discard liquid by keeping tube upright, insert pipette tip and gently push beads aside to remove liquid.

steps.title: Pour the beads into the bead...
steps.instruction: Pour the beads into the bead retainer that is in a collection tube.

steps.title: Pulse spin (≤ 1 second) the...
steps.instruction: Pulse spin (≤ 1 second) the tubes to remove any residual wash buffer from the beads.

steps.title: Separate the bead retainer from the...
steps.instruction: Separate the bead retainer from the collection tube and pour the beads into the labeled tube with 100 μL of elution buffer and save the bead retainer for a future step.

steps.title: Incubate on thermal mixer at 56...
steps.instruction: Incubate on thermal mixer at 56 °C for 00:10:00 and during incubation, make sure all sides of the beads are exposed to elution buffer by tilting the tube almost horizontally and gently shaking after the first 5 min.

steps.title: Incubate DNA by one of the...
steps.instruction: Incubate DNA by one of the following methods to make sure it is homogeneously dissolved: at 37 °C for 01:00:00 at 4 °C Overnight

steps.title: Pour the eluate and the glass...
steps.instruction: Pour the eluate and the glass beads into the bead retainer that is placed in the final labeled tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 12000 rpm, 25 °C,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 12000 rpm, 25 °C, 30 seconds to separate the eluate from the glass beads.

steps.title: Mix gently by pipetting using wide-bore tip
steps.instruction: Mix gently by pipetting using wide-bore tip.

steps.title: Follow "ONT Library Prep for Whole...
steps.instruction: Follow "ONT Library Prep for Whole Genome Sequencing" protocol to build libraries for whole genome sequencing using Oxford Nanopore platform.

steps.title: Qubit each tubes and keep on...
steps.instruction: Qubit each tubes and keep on ice until library prep or store in -20°C

steps.title: Add 900 µL DRS and mix...
steps.instruction: Add 900 µL DRS and mix by pipetting 10 times slowly up and down using a 5 mL pipette.

steps.title: Overlay with 4 mL PBS using...
steps.instruction: Overlay with 4 mL PBS using a P1000 or P5000 pipette, whichever you are more comfortable with. Place the tube on a flat surface, tilt it at a 45-degree angle, and slowly add the first 1 mL. You can increase speed gradually after the initial addition of PBS.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 3000 x g, 4...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 3000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes with no brake. Three phases will be formed: A top transparent buffer layer, A middle cloudy debris band/ring, A bottom transparent layer containing nuclei. The pellet is usually visible.

steps.title: Aspirate the two top phases (the...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the two top phases (the buffer layer and the cloudy debris band, as shown within the red box in the picture) and discard them into a designated waste container. Aspirate the first phase, then the second phase. Stay above the third layer of nuclei to prevent loss. Usually, it will take ~1000 µL to take out the debris band, and the nuclei will be suspended in the remaining ~2000 µL.

steps.title: Add cold RSB to the remaining...
steps.instruction: Add cold RSB to the remaining suspension until a final volume of 5 mL is reached. Gently invert the tube three times . DO NOT vortex.

steps.title: Prepare and label the following tubes...
steps.instruction: Prepare and label the following tubes (for each sample) for nuclei extraction: Pre-chilled gentleMACS C Tube 5 mL tube 15 mL tube MACS SmartStrainers (70 µm) MACS SmartStrainers (30 µm) Disposable Hemacytometer

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1000 x g, 4...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes with full break.

steps.title: Fully resuspend the nuclei pellet in 187
steps.instruction: Fully resuspend the nuclei pellet in 187.5 µL RSB.

steps.title: Count nuclei
steps.instruction: Count nuclei: Use 1:11 dilution factor: 2 µL sample + 20 µL dye . Mix by pipetting up and down 7-10 times. Load a 10 µL sample onto a disposable hemocytometer. * If the counts taken from two opposing squares are vastly different (i.e., one square has around half the amount of nuclei of the other square), then take a count from a third square to decide which count is the more accurate one.

steps.title: Incubate for 01:00:00 at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 01:00:00 at room temperature. During incubation, make sure all sides of the beads are exposed to elution buffer by tilting the tube almost horizontally and gently shaking every 5 min.

steps.title: Add 20% SDS to each sample...
steps.instruction: Add 20% SDS to each sample for a final concentration of 1%. Calculate the amount of 20% SDS you need. Insert the pipette tip into the solution . Add one small drop of SDS. Gently move the tip to a different location in the tube and add another drop. Repeat until the total calculated volume of 20% SDS is added. Avoid creating bubbles — the dropwise method ensures even distribution.

steps.title: Thoroughly mix by gently inverting tube
steps.instruction: Thoroughly mix by gently inverting tube. Do not vortex or pipette to mix. * If you are going to extract DNA later, flash freeze and store at -80 °C .

steps.title: Using a wide-bore tip, transfer as...
steps.instruction: Using a wide-bore tip, transfer as much of the upper phase containing the DNA to a labeled, empty 2 mL tube without disrupting the pellet at the bottom. Note the total volume for later steps.

steps.title: Mix on vertical rotating mixer for...
steps.instruction: Mix on vertical rotating mixer for 20 rpm, °C, 1 minute .

steps.title: Add 180 µL Protein Separation solution
steps.instruction: Add 180 µL Protein Separation solution .

steps.title: Centrifuge for 16000 x g, 25 °C, 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 16000 x g, 25 °C, 10 minutes .

steps.title: Incubate in a thermal mixer at...
steps.instruction: Incubate in a thermal mixer at 2000 rpm, 56 °C, 10 minutes . * If you are working from thawed reaction, first add 20 µL Proteinase k and 10 µL RNAse A to each tube. Mix using wide-bore pipette tip.

steps.title: Add 20 µL Proteinase k and...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL Proteinase k and 10 µL RNAse A to each tube. Mix using wide-bore pipette tip. * Skip this step if you are going to freeze the lysate and extract DNA later.
